message:
So last weekend my kids asked why I kept a bin of 'emergency stuff' in the hall closet, and instead of giving them the serious safety talk, I just... turned it into a game.

We pulled out the blankets, flashlights, and snacks, built the world's coziest fort in the living room, and practiced what we'd do if the power went out. My 6-year-old declared herself 'Chief Flashlight Officer.' My 9-year-old made a 'menu' from our stored crackers and water bottles.

It took maybe 20 minutes, felt more like playtime than a chore, and honestly? I learned they actually know where to find our important docs, they remember the plan, and—bonus—they're not nervous about it. They're just ready.

I think sometimes we overthink this stuff. Preparedness doesn't have to feel heavy or stressful. Sometimes it's just... cozy and practical.

Have you found a way to make safety prep feel natural (or even fun) with your family?
